manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: shawl, butterfly, porch, pepperoni, nightshirt, carton, caribou, walrus, kleenex.
Your opponent's team words are: flute, beefburger, chopstick, warthog, owl, lamp, popcorn, firetruck.
Distractor words are: ball, matchstick, photo, lamb, bed, nectarine, pitchfork.
The kill words are: bluebird.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: shawl, butterfly, porch, pepperoni, nightshirt, carton, caribou, walrus, kleenex.
Your opponent's team words are: flute, beefburger, chopstick, warthog, owl, lamp, popcorn, firetruck.
Distractor words are: ball, matchstick, photo, lamb, bed, nectarine, pitchfork.
The kill words are: bluebird.

CLUE: animal
TARGETS: caribou, walrus, butterfly

The words caribou, walrus were guessed correctly. The word warthog was guessed but is an opponent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: shawl, butterfly, porch, pepperoni, nightshirt, carton, kleenex.
Remaining words for your opponent are: flute, beefburger, chopstick, owl, lamp, popcorn, firetruck.
Remaining distractor words are: ball, matchstick, photo, lamb, bed, nectarine, pitchfork.
Remaining kill words are: bluebird.

CLUE: soft
TARGETS: shawl, nightshirt, kleenex

The word lamb was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

I need to give a clue that relates to some of these remaining words. Let me think about associations:

- shawl: clothing, soft, warm, wrap
- butterfly: insect, flying, wings, nature
- porch: house, outdoor, furniture, sitting
- pepperoni: food, pizza, meat, spicy
- nightshirt: clothing, sleep, soft, cotton
- carton: container, box, milk, juice, packaging
- kleenex: tissue, soft, paper, nose

My previous clue "soft" got shawl, nightshirt, kleenex. Those have been guessed.

Let me think of other associations:
- All can be found in a house: porch, carton, kleenex
- All are items: shawl, pepperoni, nightshirt, carton, kleenex
- All can be noun: all of them

Let me try something else:
- Butterfly and pepperoni are both types of things you might see on a pizza (butterfly as a topping? No, that's weird)
- Porch and carton - no obvious connection
- All start with different letters

Let me think of "sleep" - nightshirt, and maybe butterfly (as in dreams?), and kleenex (for runny nose at night). Also a stretch.

I'll focus on finding a connection with porch and carton. Both can be found outside a house. They're also containers or structures. Maybe I can create a clue around that concept. Porch is an outdoor space, and carton can be used for carrying things outside. I'll explore this connection further.
